checks.a_analysis: I have compared each section of the JSON output with the corresponding pages in the source PDF. The JSON content accurately reflects the headings, lists, and table data found in the original document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or section headings (other than expected normalized heading levels) were found.

I checked the contact information on the final page of the document (Section 15). While the contact handles and URL present in the source are missing from the output, there is no instance of fabricated (incorrect or invented) contact information. The output contains only the heading 'Let's Connect!' and image placeholders.

The source PDF contains 15 pages. The JSON output contains 15 sections, each corresponding to a page of the source document. No entire pages or primary sections were silently dropped during the conversion process.

I examined all tables throughout the document. For instance, the table on Page 10 (Section 10) correctly contains two data rows in both the source and the output. Other tables likewise preserve the row counts found in the source material.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Section 15 (corresponding to Page 15) is missing approximately 85% of its text content. The source document includes specific contact handles (@NCDIT, @BroadbandIO, @ncicenter, NCDIT, NC DIT), the agency name (NC Department of Information Technology), and a URL (it.nc.gov). The JSON output only preserves the heading 'Let's Connect!' and various image icons, omitting all identifying text for the contact methods.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains text labels for social media and web contact: '@NCDIT', '@BroadbandIO', '@ncicenter', 'NCDIT', 'NC Department of Information Technology', 'NC DIT', and 'it.nc.gov'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 15 contains only the heading "Let's Connect!" and several image elements, with no mention of the handles or URL.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text across the first 14 pages is remarkably faithful to the source document. No minor wording changes that alter the meaning of the goals or strategic direction were detected. The discrepancies found are limited to the major omission in the final section.

checks.a_analysis: The output was analyzed for duplicated text blocks. The recurring footer text 'Goal 1: Improve, expand, and support statewide geospatial data and applications.' and 'Goal 2. Collaborate and conduct outreach...' appear multiple times in the output (Sections 4-10 and 11-14 respectively), but these reflect intentional repetition in the source PDF as slide footers. No other duplications of main body text were identified.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistently applied across slides. Main slide titles are tagged as Level 1 headings in most sections (e.g., Sections 4, 6, 7), but switch to Level 2 headings in others (e.g., Sections 5, 12, 13) for identical structural elements. Additionally, the footer refrain text is inconsistently tagged as a Level 2 heading in some sections (Section 4, 6, 10) and as a paragraph in others (Section 5, 7, 8, etc.). This breaks the semantic structure and predictability of the document for screen reader users.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide titles like 'Goal 1.1...' (Page 4) and 'Goal 1.2...' (Page 5) are structural equivalents.
checks.b_evidence_output: In Section 4, element index 12 is tagged as heading level 1 ('text': "Goal 1.1..."). In Section 5, element index 16 is tagged as heading level 2 ('text': "Goal 1.2..."). Similar inconsistencies exist between Section 11 (H1) and Section 12 (H2).

checks.a_analysis: All tables present in the source PDF were correctly converted into the JSON output. Headers (Benefit, Action) are properly identified, and the row/column structure matches the source precisely, including the multi-row table on page 10.
The reading order within each section follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the slides. The progression from slide title to table to footer text is maintained correctly across the document.
The bulleted lists on pages 2 and 3 are faithfully represented as unordered lists in the output JSON (Sections 2 and 3).

The source PDF on page 15 contains several contact details, including the URL 'it.nc.gov'. This URL is completely missing from the output JSON. While several handles (e.g., @NCDIT) are also missing, the omission of a standard government URL is a significant oversight in link/contact accessibility.
checks.b_evidence_source: The URL 'it.nc.gov' is clearly visible on the bottom right of the 'Let’s Connect!' slide (Page 15).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 15 contains only a heading and an image; the URL text and potential link are entirely omitted.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF slides. Each page of the PDF is converted into a logical section in the HTML. The headings, tables, and footer-style goal repetitions are presented in the same order as the original document. The transition from a slide-based layout to a continuous HTML page is handled well, maintaining the intended flow of information.

The tables on pages 4 through 14 of the PDF are correctly rendered in the HTML. They maintain a two-column structure with clear 'Benefit' and 'Action' headers and properly aligned row content. The use of background shading for headers and borders makes the data easily readable and functionally equivalent to the source.

The primary meaningful image, the 'NC DIT' logo, is present and correctly rendered throughout the converted document. Although the small social media icons from the final 'Let’s Connect!' page (PDF p. 15) are not visible in the HTML, the critical information (the handles and website URL) is preserved as text. The core visual identity of the agency is maintained via the large logo instances.

No unintended visual duplication of body content was found. The repetition of the parent goals (Goal 1 and Goal 2) at the end of each sub-goal section correctly reflects the content found on the individual slides of the source PDF. The presence of titles in the navigation sidebar as well as the main body is an intentional accessibility feature.
